missing_requirements.evidence_quote: To assist and enable online shopaholics to buy products with ease (Figures 10.1–10.8).
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall provide an easy-to-use interface for online shopping.
The system shall not require any pre-conditions for registration or login.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall show more relevant results when searching within a specified website.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is not present in the source text. The original requirement only mentioned showing 'more desired and user-specific results'.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall display more relevant results when filtering products by user-selected criteria.
hallucinations.reason: This requirement is not present in the source text. The original requirement only mentioned showing 'more desired and user-specific results'.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall allow users to register and login.
duplicates.requirement_b: Allows the user to register/login to use the system.
duplicates.reason: These two requirements are similar, but not identical. The rewritten requirement is more concise.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall enable users to purchase products.
ambiguities.reason: This requirement is ambiguous because it does not specify what happens after the user selects a product. Does the system redirect them to the product's website, or does it provide a payment interface?

Source text:
<<<
This case study focuses on using web scrapping for product comparison across websites to reduce
the amount of time and effort involved in finding and comparing the products. To assist and
enable online shopaholics to buy products with ease (Figures 10.1–10.8).
1. Register
Description: Allows the user to register/login to use the system.
Flow of Events
Basic Flow: Allows into the system.
Alternative Flow: Retry to login.
Pre-Conditions: Nil.
Post-Conditions: Control goes to the search product page.
Search Product
Description: Allows the user to search for the product of their choices such as medicines,
clothes and machinery.
Flow of Events
Basic Flow: Displays the search result for the product.
Alternative Flow: Nil.
Pre-Conditions: Users must have logged in.
Post-Conditions: Nil.
3. Search Filter
Description: This allows the user to specify the website on which their product has to be
searched. Example: Search in Amazon.
Flow of Events
Basic Flow: Shows more desired and user-specific results.
Alternative Flow: Nil.
Pre-Conditions: Search product.
Post-Conditions: Nil.
Product Filter
Description: Filter the product results based on user’s choice like rating, rev
iews, and
nomber of purchases.
Flow of Events
Basic Flow: Shows more desired and user-specific results.
Alternative Flow: Nil.
Pre-Conditions: Search product.
Post-Conditions: Nil.
5. Get Product
Description: Redirects the user to the user-selected product’s website. Then, the user can
purchase the product.
Flow of Events
Basic Flow: Enables the user to purchase the product.
Alternative Flow: Nil.
Pre-Conditions: Search product.
Post-Conditions: Nil.
>>>

    "The system shall enable users to purchase products."
